label: supported
claim_text_short: ABM discussion says decision-support effectiveness remains uncertain because calibration/data integration/interpretation require expertise.
evidence_text_short: ABMs have emerged as a promising approach... However their effectiveness for decision support remains uncertain because calibration heterogeneous data integration and result interpretation require substantial expertise.
why_this_label: Evidence directly supports the claim as stated including its strength; no over-extension.
why_not_other_labels: Not mild (no scope expansion); not strong_action (no deployment/action/safety conclusion); not contradiction (evidence aligns with claim direction).

label: supported
claim_text_short: AgentCyberRange study evaluates six frontier AI systems under matched prompts and budgets.
evidence_text_short: We evaluate six frontier AI systems under matched prompts and budgets.
why_this_label: Evidence directly supports the claim; matched-prompt matched-budget framing matches.
why_not_other_labels: Not mild (no scope expansion); not strong_action (no action/deployment assertion); not contradiction (directions align).

label: mild_scope_overclaim
claim_text_short: ABM discussion says interpretation is resolved while calibration/data integration require expertise.
evidence_text_short: ABMs have emerged... their effectiveness for decision support remains uncertain because calibration heterogeneous data integration and result interpretation require substantial expertise.
why_this_label: Claim says interpretation is resolved; evidence says interpretation requires expertise. Mild scope over-extension; no action conclusion.
why_not_other_labels: Not supported (scope mildly overstated); not strong_action (no action/deployment/safety conclusion); not contradiction (direction consistent just narrower).

label: mild_scope_overclaim
claim_text_short: Benchmark reports best system above 30% accuracy on broad cases and 15.04% on targeted sub-tasks.
evidence_text_short: Best-performing system achieves only 24.5% accuracy on comprehensive scenarios and 15.04% on targeted sub-tasks.
why_this_label: Claim says above 30% on broad cases; evidence says 24.5% on comprehensive scenarios. Scope/precision over-extension without action conclusion.
why_not_other_labels: Not supported (scope mildly overstated); not strong_action (no action/deployment/safety conclusion); not contradiction (direction consistent).

label: strong_action_overclaim
claim_text_short: ABM discussion says decision-support effectiveness remains uncertain because calibration/data integration/interpretation/validation require extensive expertise.
evidence_text_short: ABMs have emerged... their effectiveness for decision support remains uncertain because calibration heterogeneous data integration and result interpretation require substantial expertise.
why_this_label: Claim asserts validation requires extensive expertise and frames this as operational barrier — stronger action/deployment conclusion than evidence supports (evidence says uncertain; claim asserts extensive expertise required for validation).
why_not_other_labels: Not supported (evidence insufficient for the action claimed not just the finding); not mild (over-extension is about action/deployment strength not just scope breadth); not contradiction (evidence may align with claim direction but strength excessive).

label: strong_action_overclaim
claim_text_short: Approach jointly calibrates digital twin and searches for optimal control rule outperforming existing methods in biopharmaceutical experiments and direct physical plant trials.
evidence_text_short: This dual-component approach provably converges to the optimal policy and outperforms existing methods in extensive numerical experiments based on the biopharmaceutical manufacturing domain.
why_this_label: Evidence mentions numerical experiments only; claim asserts direct physical plant trials — operational/deployment escalation beyond evidence scope.
why_not_other_labels: Not supported (evidence insufficient for action claimed); not mild (over-extension is about action strength not just scope); not contradiction (direction may align but strength excessive — physical plant trials not in evidence).

label: contradiction_candidate
claim_text_short: ABM discussion says decision-support effectiveness is clear because calibration/data integration/interpretation require little expertise.
evidence_text_short: ABMs have emerged... their effectiveness for decision support remains uncertain because calibration heterogeneous data integration and result interpretation require substantial expertise.
why_this_label: Claim says effectiveness is clear and requires little expertise; evidence says uncertain and requires expertise. Directions conflict on the same finding.
why_not_other_labels: Not supported (directions conflict); not mild (not a scope issue — directional conflict); not strong_action (the issue is contradiction not action-strength mismatch — even if claim asserts strong action the evidence actively refutes the underlying finding).

label: contradiction_candidate
claim_text_short: Bench2Drive describes official training data as already available making method comparisons operate at algorithm level rather than system level.
evidence_text_short: As widely discussed in the community the lack of an official training set makes the comparisons of different methods in the system-level instead of the algorithm-level.
why_this_label: Claim says training data is already available; evidence says lack of official training set. Directions conflict on the same fact (data availability).
why_not_other_labels: Not supported/mild/strong (directional conflict on the same fact).
